summary: The review of "CDA example 3.pdf" reveals a high-risk posture due to multiple red-flagged clauses that deviate materially from the University of Auckland's Preferred Contracting Positions. Key issues include incomplete or ambiguous confidentiality provisions, lack of alignment with UoA's standards for exclusions, duration, and disclosure rights, and problematic dispute resolution and governing law terms. Immediate escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is recommended to address these deficiencies and ensure compliance with institutional standards.

flags.clause_title: 1 Introduction. The Parties have entered or propose to enter discussions
flags.snippet: 1 Introduction. The Parties have entered or propose to enter discussions option destroys all documents containing Confidential Information and any other in order to explore mutually beneficial business opportunities (th…
flags.rationale: The clause touches on Confidentiality but includes problematic language regarding the destruction of documents containing Confidential Information. This deviates materially from UoA's Preferred Position #POS-11, which requires the definition of confidential information to exclude public, independently developed, or previously known information, and allows UoA to disclose to agents and Affiliates. Additionally, the clause does not specify a duration for confidentiality obligations, which is required to align with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable tiers. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required to address these issues.

flags.clause_title: 3 Use of Confidential Information.
flags.snippet: 3 Use of Confidential Information. partnership of any kind between the Parties.
flags.rationale: The clause text does not provide any substantive details about the use, definition, or limitations of confidential information, nor does it align with UoA Position #POS-11. Specifically, it fails to exclude information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, or legally required to be disclosed. It also does not address UoA's ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates or specify a confidentiality duration. This omission materially deviates from the Preferred and Acceptable tiers, requiring escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review and amendment.

flags.clause_title: 3.1 The Recipient shall keep Confidential Information strictly
flags.snippet: 3.1 The Recipient shall keep Confidential Information strictly
flags.rationale: The clause text is incomplete and does not provide sufficient detail to confirm alignment with UoA Position #POS-11 on Confidentiality. Specifically, it lacks key elements such as the definition of Confidential Information (e.g., exclusions for public information, independently developed information, etc.), the ability for UoA to disclose to agents and Affiliates, and the duration of confidentiality obligations. Without these details, the clause materially deviates from the Preferred and Acceptable tiers and requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for review.
flags.text: 3.1 The Recipient shall keep Confidential Information strictly

flags.clause_title: 3.3 The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information only to
flags.snippet: 3.3 The Recipient may disclose the Confidential Information only to the Recipient or any third party arising out of the correctness or to its employees, agents, consultants, contractors, and those of completeness of the Confidential Information.
flags.rationale: The clause is unclear and ambiguous, particularly with the phrase 'arising out of the correctness or to its employees, agents, consultants, contractors, and those of completeness of the Confidential Information.' This wording does not align with UoA Position #POS-11, which requires a clear definition of confidential information and specifies that UoA must be able to disclose to agents and Affiliates. Additionally, the clause does not address the exclusion of public, independently developed, or previously known information, nor does it specify a time limit for confidentiality obligations. This falls outside both the Preferred and Acceptable tiers and requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts.

flags.clause_title: “Purpose”). In connection with the Purpose, either Party may disclose
flags.snippet: “Purpose”). In connection with the Purpose, either Party may disclose or relating to Confidential Information. Upon the request of the disclosing Party, an information of a confidential nature to the other Party which is then officer of the Recipient shall promptly certify in writing that such destruction has been completed.
flags.rationale: The clause addresses Confidentiality but does not align with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions. It lacks key exclusions for public information, independently developed information, previously known information, and legally required disclosures. Additionally, it does not specify a time limit for confidentiality obligations (e.g., up to 5 years post-completion). The requirement for certification of destruction is overly prescriptive and may impose administrative burdens. Escalation to Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required.

flags.clause_title: 4 Right to Notice. In the event the Recipient receives a request to release
flags.snippet: 4 Right to Notice. In the event the Recipient receives a request to release validity or termination, shall be determined by arbitration in London in
flags.rationale: The clause touches on Dispute Resolution but does not align with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions. Arbitration in London is inconsistent with the Preferred position, which requires escalation to senior management, mediation, and proceedings in English, with a process that does not prevent urgent interlocutory action through courts. The clause also does not meet the Acceptable position, as it does not appear to be tied to a NZ or International Funding Agency contract where dispute clauses cannot be altered. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts, or General Counsel is required.

flags.clause_title: 7 Term. Either Party may terminate this Agreement on thirty (30) days’
flags.snippet: 7 Term. Either Party may terminate this Agreement on thirty (30) days’ confidential and not disclose such Confidential Information to
flags.rationale: The clause combines termination and confidentiality topics, but the text is incomplete and ambiguous. It does not clearly define confidentiality obligations, exclusions, or duration, nor does it align with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions for confidentiality (POS-11). Additionally, termination provisions are unclear and do not match UoA's Preferred position (POS-19), which requires mutual termination for material breach or insolvency, and clear rights for UoA to terminate for non-payment. Escalation to Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required for both confidentiality and termination issues.

flags.snippet: 8 Disclaimer. The disclosing Party discloses the Confidential Information
flags.rationale: The clause text is incomplete and does not provide sufficient detail to assess alignment with UoA's Preferred or Acceptable positions on Confidentiality (POS-11). Specifically, it lacks any definition of confidential information, exclusions for public or independently developed information, or a time limit for confidentiality obligations. This creates ambiguity and potential risk for UoA. Escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required to address these deficiencies.

flags.clause_title: 11 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and in accordance
flags.snippet: 11 Governing Law. This Agreement shall be governed by and in accordance been independently developed by the Recipient without
flags.rationale: The clause text is incomplete and does not clearly specify the governing law or jurisdiction, which is a critical requirement under UoA Position #POS-16. The clause must explicitly state the governing law as New Zealand (preferred) or one of the acceptable jurisdictions (Australia, the United Kingdom, Common European countries, Singapore, or non-exclusive jurisdiction of a specific US state for US-based clients). Escalation to General Counsel is required to resolve ambiguity and ensure compliance.

flags.clause_title: without any warranty as to the correctness or completeness of the
flags.snippet: without any warranty as to the correctness or completeness of the the Purpose and shall not use the Confidential Information for Confidential Information, express or implied, of any kind. The disclosing any other purpos…
flags.rationale: This clause touches on Liability Limitations and Exclusions but deviates materially from UoA's Preferred and Acceptable positions. It broadly excludes liability for 'any costs or damages whatsoever incurred,' which is overly expansive and does not align with UoA's Preferred position limiting liability to direct losses caused by negligence or wilful misconduct, capped at NZD$500,000 or contract value. Furthermore, it does not explicitly exclude consequential or indirect losses, nor does it align with the Acceptable carve-outs for liability caps. Escalation to the Research Grants and Contracts Delegations is required to assess compliance and negotiate revisions.

flags.rationale: The clause text is fragmented and unclear, mixing confidentiality obligations with arbitration rules. It does not align with UoA Position #POS-11, which requires clear exclusions for public information, independently developed information, and legally required disclosures. Additionally, the clause does not specify the duration of confidentiality obligations or UoA's ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates. This deviation falls outside the Acceptable tier and requires escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts for clarification and potential redrafting.

flags.clause_title: 2 Confidential information. As used in this Agreement, “Confidential
flags.snippet: 2 Confidential information. As used in this Agreement, Confidential
flags.rationale: The clause touches on the topic of Confidentiality, which is sensitive under UoA Position #POS-11. However, the exact alignment with the Preferred or Acceptable tier is unclear from the snippet provided. The clause must be reviewed to ensure it excludes information already public, independently developed, previously known by the receiving party, and information legally required to be disclosed. Additionally, UoA must retain the ability to disclose to agents and Affiliates, and the confidentiality duration should not exceed 5 years post-completion. If these conditions are not met, escalation to the Senior Manager, Research Grants and Contracts is required.

flags.rationale: The clause addresses confidentiality obligations but deviates from UoA's Preferred Position. Specifically, the confidentiality duration is limited to one year post-termination, which is shorter than the Preferred Position of up to 5 years. However, it aligns with the Acceptable tier, which allows confidentiality obligations limited in time (e.g., 5–7 years post-termination/expiry/disclosure). The clause also includes provisions for trade secrets, which are maintained confidentially as long as they remain trade secrets, aligning with standard practices. Escalation is not required unless further clarification is needed.

flags.clause_title: Information” means either Party’s non-public and/or proprietary
flags.snippet: Information” means either Party’s non-public and/or proprietary but not limited to copyright, trademarks, patents and any other information that: (a) if disclosed in writing, is labelled as “confidential” intellectual p…
flags.rationale: The clause includes references to intellectual property rights (e.g., copyright, trademarks, patents), which fall under the scope of Intellectual Property. UoA Contracting Positions explicitly state that all IP-related clauses must be referred to Auckland UniServices Ltd (Head of IP) for review. This clause does not materially deviate from the requirement to escalate IP matters, but it cannot be assessed for compliance without UniServices input.

flags.clause_title: CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT
flags.snippet: CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT This Confidentiality Agreement (the Agreement) is entered into as of the date of the last signature (Effective Date) by and between registered in England and Wales under Company No. and the
flags.rationale: The clause text provided does not address the substantive confidentiality obligations, exclusions, or duration, which are the key elements covered under UoA Position #POS-11. Instead, it appears to be introductory boilerplate language establishing the agreement's parties and effective date. As such, it does not fall within the scope of the UoA Preferred Contracting Positions or the Acceptable tier for confidentiality. This clause requires further review to ensure substantive confidentiality terms are included elsewhere in the contract.
flags.text:
CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT
This Confidentiality Agreement (the “Agreement”) is entered into as of the date of the last signature (“Effective Date”) by and between registered
in England and Wales under Company No. and the entity or individual listed in the signature block below (“Partner”). and
Partner are each a “Party” and collectively, the “Parties.”

The United Kingdom is explicitly listed as an acceptable jurisdiction. No escalation is required as the clause aligns with pre-approved deviations.
